title: Previous awareness under anesthesia documentation

treatment_and_management.text: Supports a structured interview after a reported event, documenting the account, offering counseling or psychological support, and completing an occurrence report without confirming causation.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The post-event section supports documenting severity or impact, an already completed structured interview, occurrence report, or offered support without asserting causation.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports a structured interview after a reported event, documenting the account, offering counseling or psychological support, and completing an occurrence report without confirming causation.

source.title: Practice Advisory for Intraoperative Awareness and Brain Function Monitoring

follow_up.text: Supports review of records, interview about previous anesthesia experiences including prior awareness, focused risk assessment, and informing selected higher-risk patients without predicting awareness.
follow_up.rationale: The ASA advisory supports documenting a patient-reported previous awareness experience, related history, focused risk review, and selected discussion without confirming the event or predicting recurrence.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of records, interview about previous anesthesia experiences including prior awareness, focused risk assessment, and informing selected higher-risk patients without predicting awareness.

source.title: Practice Advisory for Intraoperative Awareness and Brain Function Monitoring

source_limitations: Procedure and anesthetic details, timing and content of recall, distress, prior records, risk factors, corroboration, interview findings, occurrence report, counseling, psychological impact, plan, consent, monitoring, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
